nodes.name: Documentation gap
nodes.summary: The repository currently lacks a root README in the working tree; .understand-anything adds onboarding docs without touching that deletion.

nodes.name: Duplicated settings diff logic
nodes.summary: TenantConverter and TenantFacadeChangeDetectionService both encode legacy defaults and repeated feature-toggle JSON checks.

nodes.name: External service synchronization
nodes.summary: Tenant create/update/search flows coordinate with ConsultingTypeService, ApplicationSettingsService, and UserAdminService.

nodes.name: Keycloak JWT role authorization
nodes.summary: JWT realm roles are mapped into local AUTHORIZATION_* authorities and enforced mainly with @PreAuthorize annotations.

nodes.name: MariaDB and Liquibase persistence
nodes.summary: TenantEntity maps to the tenant table and Liquibase changelogs describe historical schema evolution.

nodes.name: OpenAPI-first REST boundary
nodes.summary: The public TenantService API is described in api/tenantservice.yaml and implemented by TenantController through generated Spring interfaces.

nodes.name: Authorization workaround risk
nodes.summary: TenantFacadeAuthorisationService contains a temporary technical-user bypass in canAccessTenant.

nodes.name: Stale dependency stack risk
nodes.summary: Spring Boot 3.0.0, old Keycloak adapters, Springfox, Springdoc, and javax.ws.rs create an upgrade and compatibility hotspot.

nodes.name: Runtime configuration validation
nodes.summary: ConfigurationValidator fails startup when database, Keycloak, JWT, and service URL settings are missing.

nodes.name: Single-domain multitenancy override
nodes.summary: Single-domain deployments can override subdomain behavior and selected legal/privacy content from ApplicationSettingsService.

nodes.name: Tenant resolution strategy
nodes.summary: Tenant context is resolved from an authenticated token first, then cookie state in single-domain mode, then subdomain lookup.

nodes.name: Tenant settings and legal content governance
nodes.summary: TenantSetting and TenantContent rules restrict which roles may change feature toggles, privacy, terms, and impressum content.

nodes.name: Unused contracts and helpers
nodes.summary: services/agencyadminservice.yaml is only referenced through useradminservice.yaml schemas, and TenantHeaderSupplier has no active call site in the scanned code.

nodes.name: ORISO Integrations
nodes.summary: Coordinates TenantService behavior with ConsultingTypeService, ApplicationSettingsService, and UserAdminService.

businessRules: Tenant creation rolls back if default consulting type creation fails.
Single-domain main tenant subdomain is stored centrally.
Admin search enriches tenant rows with admin email data.

nodes.name: Tenant Access Control
nodes.summary: Maps Keycloak roles and JWT tenant claims to local authorities and tenant access decisions.

businessRules: Super admin is tenantId 0 plus tenant-admin role.
Single-tenant admins can update only allowed settings/content.
Public restricted tenant endpoints are permitAll but return limited data.
crossDomainInteractions: Keycloak realm roles feed local authorization.

nodes.name: Tenant Registry
nodes.summary: Owns tenant records, subdomains, licensing limits, theming, settings JSON, and multilingual legal content.

businessRules: Tenant 0 is technical and excluded from admin search.
Tenant create requires id to be null.
Subdomain uniqueness is enforced except for single-domain override mode.
crossDomainInteractions: ConsultingType defaults are created for each tenant.

tour.title: Start at the HTTP contract
tour.description: api/tenantservice.yaml is the source of truth for the service API. TenantController implements the generated interfaces and adds a handwritten DELETE /tenant/{id} endpoint.

tour.title: Follow the facade
tour.description: TenantServiceFacade is the main orchestration point. It validates input, checks authorization, persists tenants, resolves restricted reads, and calls downstream ORISO services.

tour.title: Understand tenant authorization
tour.description: WebSecurityConfig authenticates JWTs, JwtAuthConverter maps Keycloak roles, and TenantFacadeAuthorisationService checks tenant-specific field changes.

tour.title: Understand tenant context
tour.description: TenantResolverService prefers authenticated token tenant claims, falls back to cookies in single-domain mode, then subdomain lookup for public requests.

tour.title: Trace ORISO integrations
tour.description: ConsultingTypeService, ApplicationSettingsService, and UserAdminService are wrapper services around generated OpenAPI clients and carry Authorization/CSRF headers downstream.

tour.title: Review data and deployment
tour.description: TenantEntity maps the tenant table; Liquibase changelogs maintain schema history. Runtime behavior is controlled through Spring profile properties, Dockerfile, and ConfigurationValidator.

tour.title: Check the risk map
tour.description: The highest-maintenance areas are old Keycloak/Springfox dependencies, duplicated settings logic, a temporary technical-user access workaround, and deployment/config drift.
